title: Interactive Proofs
description: Interactive proofs model verification as a dialogue between a prover and a verifier. They matter because many zero-knowledge protocols start from this challenge-response structure before later non-interactive variants compress the interaction.

resources.title: MIT OCW: Lecture 25 - Interactive Proof Systems, IP
resources.notes: Structured lecture notes on prover-verifier protocols, completeness, and soundness.

resources.title: Proofs, Arguments, and Zero-Knowledge
resources.notes: Accessible bridge from interactive proofs to modern argument systems and zero-knowledge.

resources.title: Inventing Bitcoin
resources.notes: Beginner-friendly conceptual bridge into the mechanics behind this topic.
